floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: the if((flag&&flag)&&flag){do{}while(0);} wrapper is very likely FULLY INERT -- all three short-circuit branches are jump1 branch-to-following-insn deletions (empty body => target l

frontier.hypothesis: Wholesale deletion of src/main.c:2889-2891 byte-matches ONLY IF the do-while's mid-block sched fence (sched.c:2067-2088) is not what holds `lw $a0` (s:12-13) below the two macro-sw stores (s:8-11). The if-wrapper part is inert regardless (jump1 deletion). Expectation revised by same-session correction: divergence plausible; still run first as the judge-mandated clean-form necessity probe.
frontier.mechanism: jump.c jump1 branch-to-following-insn deletion removes all three short-circuit branches before cse/flow/RA (cse.c cannot fold symbol_ref!=0 -- #pragma weak #if 0 at cse.c:5375-5378), and the leftover empty LOOP_BEG/END note pair encloses zero insns so no pass (flow loop_depth weighting, jump LABEL_OUTSIDE_LOOP_P, reorg) consumes it.
frontier.next_probe: Delete src/main.c:2889-2891, `& tools/wteng.ps1 main sandbox func_8008C184 --disable all` (expect 0), then verify-oracle + queue done.

frontier.hypothesis: HIGH-PRIOR CLOSER (revised): a STANDALONE `do { } while (0); /* FAKE: sched.c mid-block loop-note fence keeps the D_800A3044 load chain below the AF4/AF0 stores */` at the same spot (no if-wrapper) reproduces the fence and byte-matches.
frontier.mechanism: sched.c:2067-2088 mid-block LOOP-note fence (full dependence wall in sched1+sched2) preserving source store->load order; reorg.c bnez delay fill unaffected.
frontier.next_probe: Read the failing sandbox diff to classify the divergence site (bnez delay vs sched placements), then re-add the standalone annotated do-while and re-sandbox; sanctioned single-level form per do-while-zero-exception.md.

frontier.hypothesis: If the standalone do-while ALSO fails, the wrapper's transient pre-jump1 branches themselves perturbed early RTL generation (stmt.c expand state or jump1 ordering side-effects) in a way the notes alone do not.
frontier.mechanism: expand/stmt.c RTL-generation context and jump.c first-pass processing order -- the only phases where the deleted branches ever existed.
frontier.next_probe: cc1 -dj dumps of both forms (wrapper vs standalone) on the preprocessed TU; diff the .jump dumps to locate the first divergent insn, then derive a real-statement lever from that site.

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): Cheat construct at src/main.c:2889-2891: `if ((flag && flag) && flag) { do { } while (0); }` — flag is always non-null (&D_800F1AEC), so the condition is always true and the body emits nothing; this is a compound `if(1){...}` always-true wrapper (explicitly forbidden family) enclosing an un-annotated do-while-zero outside its narrow sanctioned prerequisites. Additionally, the triple `(flag && flag) && flag` evaluation bumps reg_n_refs for the flag pseudo (combine-foldable chain-extender family). Next action: redo the function — remove the compound construct and find a clean C structure that produces the correct reorg.c scheduling (either via structural changes that naturally fill the delay slot, or — only after documented lever exhaustion and with LABEL_OUTSIDE_LOOP_P purpose confirmed — a standalone `do{}while(0)` with `/* FAKE */` annotation, NOT wrapped in if(1)).  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)
